danger_signals.visual_form: Organic creature designs with hostile postures; aggressive animation frames
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental threat; biological corruption of normal fauna

danger_signals.visual_form: Large arachnid form, visually distinct from standard enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Source of infestation; apex threat to be neutralized

danger_signals.visual_form: Life bar made of orbs that decrease visually when damage taken
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate danger to Vectorman; proximity to failure state

danger_signals.visual_form: Hot surfaces indicated through level design context requiring scorpion morph to traverse safely
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental danger requiring specific adaptation or morph ability

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible orbs scattered through levels that replenish life bar
Collectable items that modify shooting mechanism and increase damage
safety_signals.meaning: Power progression; player empowerment for upcoming threats

safety_signals.visual_form: End of level areas where Vectorman reaches exit
Vectorman transformation after defeating specific enemy types (scorpion, rhinoceros beetle)
safety_signals.meaning: Adaptive capability unlocked; environmental mastery available

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Active protagonist moving through modernized environments

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Organic creature designs; biological corruption aesthetic
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Scorpion enemies (traversable on hot surfaces), Rhinoceros beetle enemies (ramming capability), Black Widow Queen (boss form)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence indicates biological contamination; marks territory as infected
Modern technological settings as primary environment contrast to post-apocalyptic first game setting

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Post-Vectorman 1 recovery - Earth saved but vulnerable
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Modern settings (non-post-apocalyptic); research facilities and developed areas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Vectorman must re-engage with threat after ship destruction; defensive posture against new biological menace

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mutant insects present throughout levels; organic enemy design aesthetic replacing robotic enemies from first game
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies behave aggressively; Vectorman forced into active elimination mission

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hot surfaces and hostile terrain encoded in level design; require specific morphs to navigate safely
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must acquire specific enemy morphs to access certain areas; non-linear progression gates

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Weapon upgrades and health orbs visible as collectibles in environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player becomes progressively more capable; difficulty scaling through resource availability

environmental_storytelling.example: Shift from post-apocalyptic wasteland (game 1) to modern technological settings communicates world recovery and new threat emergence in seemingly safe environments
Organic insect designs instead of robotic enemies signals biological threat origin rather than mechanical corruption
Old research facility as landing point and initial exploration area implies scientific origin or involvement in infestation source
Black Widow Queen identified as source infestation suggests hive-like biological structure and parasitic nature of threat
Hot surfaces requiring scorpion morph to traverse safely communicates environmental specialization and adaptive gameplay

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Running, jumping, shooting projectiles in straight line; traversing levels left-to-right
Able to walk on hot surfaces without damage; specialized environmental access unlocked
Equipped with large horned head for ramming into enemies; melee combat capability unlocked
Aggressive movement patterns; attack Vectorman on contact; attempt to damage player
Final level confrontation; distinct behavioral patterns indicating apex threat status

notes: Vectorman 2 uses visual language shift from mechanical (game 1) to organic (game 2) to communicate narrative threat transformation. Pre-rendered 3D sprite aesthetic distinguishes protagonist from environment. Morph system tied to enemy defeat creates visual feedback loop: defeat enemy → acquire visual transformation → unlock environmental access. Health orb system provides constant visual feedback on player vulnerability state. Level design encodes environmental storytelling through hazard placement requiring specific morphs.
